text: Simplest approach: number notes sequentially in 'log' output (Note-1, Note-2, ...) based on display order, rather than changing the storage format. Event files are already ordered by timestamp filename. This keeps storage simple and gives stable references as long as notes aren't deleted (which they can't be today).

I think I'd like the persistence of an ID more. prevents inconsistency whenever notes are merged together from different sources. that would then become in cuplicate use of numbers, of course. hmmm. maybe this needs more discussing. any ideas?

On the merge-conflict problem: sequential per-ticket counter assigned at write time (count existing kind=note events + 1) is simplest and human-friendly. Yes, parallel merges can produce duplicate numbers, but: (a) two people noting the same ticket simultaneously is rare, (b) when it happens, timestamp + content disambiguates, (c) alternatives (UUIDs, hashes, timestamp-IDs) are ugly to reference verbally. Could also consider actor-prefixed numbering (e.g. 'claude-3', 'dpiegdon-5') which avoids collisions across actors — the main real-world merge scenario. Tradeoff: slightly longer to type but collision-proof for the common case.
T-79 makes this more valuable: once users can browse richer timeline/history output, stable note identifiers become the natural way to reference a specific entry from another note, chat, or review.

Proposed solution: assign a sequential note_id at write time — count existing kind=note events in the JSONL + 1 — and persist it as a 'note_id' field in the event entry. Human-friendly ('Note 17'), stable (append-only log means numbers never shift), and only collides on simultaneous same-ticket writes (extremely rare). Doctor command (T-78) can detect duplicate note_ids if they ever occur. Display in show/history as 'Note 3: some text'. This avoids UUIDs, actor-prefixes, and computed indices while giving stable addressability.

maybe we can use the comment-index in the file as note-id, and just print it in all note-outputting commands? then its calculated on the fly. no need to change storage format here, right? since comments are append-only anyway, that should also be safe and even would avoid possible conflict. json output format would require augmentation during output though.
